target.title: feat(mattermost): add /model dialog picker
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Mattermost `/oc_model` and `/oc_models` still relied on the older message picker, so they could not use Mattermost's richer dialog flow.
- Why it matters: the Discord picker already feels native and multi-step; Mattermost needed a comparable native picker path instead of staying button-first.
- What changed: plain `/oc_model` and `/oc_models` now open a Mattermost dialog when `trigger_id` is available, dialog refresh/submit callbacks stay inside the Mattermost extension, and the final apply still routes through the existing shared `/model` command path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no shared interactive-core expansion, no unrelated config/schema work, and the older message picker still remains as fallback when a dialog cannot be opened.

## Links

- Issue/PR refs: none

## Verification

- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts`
- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` and resolved the single overlapping `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.ts` import conflict from current main.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost native `/oc_model` slash-command routing and dialog callback reachability on the live devbox-to-vp deployment that backs this plugin test setup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running from this worktree on `devbox`, published through Tailscale Serve at `https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net`; Mattermost running on `vp` and calling the gateway over that public tailnet URL.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Restart the gateway from the `codex-mm-model-dialog` worktree on `devbox`.
  2. Let Mattermost native `oc_*` commands register against `https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command`.
  3. Probe the slash callback from the Mattermost host and verify it responds as a live POST-only endpoint.
  4. Run plain `/oc_model` in the Mattermost workspace tied to that deployment.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime logs and copied live output from the real deployment.

```text
11:37:01 [mattermost] registered command /oc_model (id=rr9eyu8daif6p87czrtkwxb6fe)
11:37:02 [mattermost] slash commands activated for account default (8 commands)
11:37:02 [mattermost] slash commands registered (8 commands across 1 teams, callback=https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command)
```

```text
$ curl -k -sS --connect-timeout 5 --max-time 10 -D - https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command
HTTP/2 405
allow: POST
```

- Observed result after fix: The live deployment registered native Mattermost commands against the reachable devbox callback URL instead of the old `localhost` route, the callback was reachable from the Mattermost VPS, and the live tester on that deployment reported `/oc_model` working after the routing and channel-lookup fixes. This follow-up branch update also carries the same verified slash channel facts through dialog submit when later channel lookup is forbidden.
- What was not tested: A fresh screenshot or video of the post-rebase dialog submit flow is not attached here; the new submit-fallback behavior itself is locked by the added regression coverage in `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts` and `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`, and the broader rebased Mattermost shard including `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts` and `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts` stays green.

## Behavior Notes

- The new picker is Mattermost-owned UI: dialog open, refresh, and submit live in `extensions/mattermost/**`.
- If Mattermost does not provide a usable `trigger_id`, OpenClaw falls back to the existing message-based picker instead of breaking `/oc_model`.
- Slash-command authorization now falls back to channel information already present in the slash payload when `GET /channels/<id>` is forbidden, and dialog submit now reuses the signed fallback channel snapshot for the same restricted-channel case.

target.title: fix: bound gateway session memory after compaction
target.body:
## Summary
- bound Pi SessionManager in-memory transcript retention in OpenClaw runtime after compaction
- route embedded runner, CLI runner, and CLI compaction SessionManager usage through the bounded OpenClaw wrapper
- keep full JSONL transcript persistence intact while pruning stale pre-compaction entries from live memory
- preserve clearer same-process port diagnostics

## Why
Current `main` uses `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.74.0`, whose `SessionManager` keeps every `fileEntries` entry and `byId` entry resident in live memory for long-running gateway sessions. For Paperclip/OpenClaw gateway runs, old pre-compaction transcript branches can stay resident and push gateway memory upward over time.

This PR avoids a pnpm package patch. Instead, OpenClaw installs an idempotent runtime wrapper around Pi `SessionManager` and uses it from the gateway/CLI runtime paths that open active session managers. The wrapper keeps the active branch from the latest compaction boundary forward, preserves latest state/custom metadata and retained labels, rebuilds indexes, and leaves the append-only JSONL file untouched on disk.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Long-running OpenClaw gateway/Paperclip sessions can keep stale pre-compaction Pi `SessionManager.fileEntries` and `byId` entries resident in memory. This branch prunes stale pre-compaction live memory through OpenClaw's bounded SessionManager wrapper while preserving the retained branch, compaction summary, current state/custom metadata, labels, and full persisted JSONL transcript.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at PR head `0dc0ab3173fb6940617d4fbce2e769b0b5b2388b`, using the branch's OpenClaw wrapper import `./src/agents/pi-session-manager-memory-bound.ts` over published `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.74.0`. The proof used real persisted JSONL session storage under a temporary session directory. `PI_SESSION_MANAGER_MAX_IN_MEMORY_ENTRIES=256` was set to make the retention behavior observable in a short live run.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval '...'` from the OpenClaw worktree. The command imported `SessionManager` from `./src/agents/pi-session-manager-memory-bound.ts`, created a real persisted session directory, appended 300 pre-compaction message entries, appended thinking/model/session/custom state, appended a compaction entry with a retained boundary, appended post-compaction tail messages, then inspected live manager entries, resolved context, retained labels, and the persisted JSONL transcript.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from that live command:

```json
{
  "proof": "OPENCLAW_REAL_BEHAVIOR_PROOF",
  "branchHead": "0dc0ab3173fb6940617d4fbce2e769b0b5b2388b",
  "importPath": "./src/agents/pi-session-manager-memory-bound.ts",
  "piPackage": "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent@0.74.0",
  "maxInMemoryEntries": "256",
  "entriesBeforeCompaction": 300,
  "entriesAfterCompactionInMemory": 48,
  "persistedJsonlLines": 349,
  "staleEntryInMemory": false,
  "thinkingEntryInMemory": true,
  "modelEntryInMemory": true,
  "sessionInfoEntryInMemory": true,
  "customEntryInMemory": true,
  "firstKeptEntryInMemory": true,
  "compactionEntryInMemory": true,
  "keptLabel": "kept marker",
  "sessionName": "Memory leak proof",
  "contextThinkingLevel": "high",
  "contextHasSummary": true,
  "contextHasKeptUser": true,
  "contextHasTail": true,
  "contextHasStaleRawMessage": false,
  "persistedTranscriptHasStaleRawMessage": true,
  "persistedTranscriptHasTail": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale raw pre-compaction entry was removed from live memory and no longer appeared in the built session context. The first retained entry, compaction entry, current thinking/model/session/custom metadata, retained label, compaction summary, and post-compaction tail stayed available. The persisted JSONL transcript still contained both the stale historical raw message and the post-compaction tail, so disk history was preserved while live memory was bounded.

**What was not tested:** No full gateway soak test was run on this exact PR head. The live proof directly exercises the exact OpenClaw runtime wrapper now used by the embedded runner, CLI runner, and CLI compaction paths, and automated tests cover the regression path and port diagnostics.

## Tests
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-session-manager-memory.test.ts src/infra/ports.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm deps:patches:check`
- `git diff --check && node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`

target.title: fix(gateway): recover failed lanes with missing transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A fresh per-channel session lane with `status: "failed"` could keep reusing a `sessionId` whose persisted `sessionFile` no longer exists.
- Why it matters: Channel follow-up messages can repeatedly attempt to resume an unrecoverable CLI session and effectively hang/drop instead of recovering the lane.
- What changed: Gateway agent session reuse now treats failed sessions with a missing stored transcript file as unrecoverable, rotates to a fresh session id, and clears the stale `sessionFile` through the existing rotation path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Fresh non-failed sessions, failed sessions with an existing transcript file, explicit reset flows, and session retention/pruning policy behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83488
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Failed per-channel session lanes whose stored transcript file is missing should recover by allocating a fresh session instead of resuming the dead `sessionId`.
- Real environment tested: Linux 6.8.0 host, clean Docker `node:24-bookworm` container, repository worktree mounted at `/tmp/openclaw-83488-repro`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a real OpenClaw gateway in the clean container with isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; seeded `sessions.json` with a fresh `status: "failed"` lane whose relative `sessionFile` (`missing-transcript.jsonl`) did not exist; ran `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --message proof --timeout 1 --json`; inspected the persisted session entry after the gateway handled the request.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the real gateway/CLI run:

```text
gateway-log-tail:
2026-05-18T10:39:10.571+00:00 [gateway] http server listening (...)
2026-05-18T10:39:11.377+00:00 [gateway] ready

before:
{"sessionId":"failed-missing-session-id","status":"failed","sessionFile":"missing-transcript.jsonl","missing":true}

after:
{"sessionId":"962c6140-6dc1-4d33-873d-5c43ae6a1152","rotated":true,"sessionFile":"/tmp/oc-state/agents/main/sessions/962c6140-6dc1-4d33-873d-5c43ae6a1152.jsonl"}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real gateway/CLI run did not reuse `failed-missing-session-id`; it persisted a fresh session id (`962c6140-6dc1-4d33-873d-5c43ae6a1152`) and pointed `sessionFile` at the new transcript path.
- What was not tested: Live Discord plugin E2E hang/recovery; this proof exercises the gateway lane recovery behavior directly through the OpenClaw gateway and CLI.
- Before evidence:

```text
FAIL src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts > gateway agent handler > repro #83488: failed session with missing transcript is resumed instead of rotated
AssertionError: expected 'failed-missing-session-id' not to be 'failed-missing-session-id'
```

Supplemental validation after this patch:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       100 passed (100)
All matched files use the correct format.
pnpm tsgo:test:src exited 0
```

I also posted the source-level reproduction details on the issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83488#issuecomment-4476462085

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Gateway session reuse only considered freshness (`updatedAt` / lifecycle timestamps) and the presence of `entry.sessionId`; a `failed` lane whose transcript file had disappeared could still be considered fresh and reusable.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no guard that invalidated a failed session when its stored `sessionFile` pointed at a missing file.
- Contributing context (if known): Per-channel lane recovery keeps session ids stable for fresh lanes, but this edge case needs to treat the persisted lane as unrecoverable.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `status: "failed"` + missing `sessionFile` must allocate a fresh `sessionId` and clear the stale transcript path.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The gateway agent handler is where `canReuseSession` decides whether to resume or rotate the session id.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing stale-session coverage rotates old sessions, but not fresh failed sessions whose transcript file is gone.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users on channel sessions can recover automatically from this failed-lane/missing-transcript state instead of repeatedly attempting to resume an unrecoverable session.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[channel message] -> fresh failed lane + missing transcript -> resume old sessionId -> hang/drop risk

After:
[channel message] -> failed lane + missing transcript -> rotate sessionId -> fresh run
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0
- Runtime/container: Docker `node:24-bookworm`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Source-level gateway handler reproduction for per-channel lanes
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Seed a gateway session entry with `sessionId`, `status: "failed"`, fresh lifecycle timestamps, and a `sessionFile` path that does not exist.
2. Invoke the gateway agent handler for the same session key.
3. Inspect the `agentCommand` call and persisted session entry.

### Expected

- The failed/missing-transcript lane is treated as unrecoverable.
- A new session id is allocated.
- The stale `sessionFile` is cleared.

### Actual

- Before this patch, the handler reused `failed-missing-session-id`.
- After this patch, the handler rotates the session id and clears `sessionFile`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Source-level reproduction fails on current behavior.
  - Focused regression passes after the fix.
  - Full gateway agent handler test file passes (`99 passed`).
  - Touched files pass formatter check.
  - Source test TypeScript check passes (`pnpm tsgo:test:src`).
  - Local `codex review --base origin/main` returned: `No prioritized, actionable findings were identified.`
- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing stale-session rotation test still passes as part of the full file.
  - The new guard only applies when `entry.status === "failed"` and the stored `sessionFile` path is non-empty and missing.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Live Discord plugin E2E hang/recovery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A failed session whose transcript file was intentionally removed will no longer be resumed.
  - Mitigation: That is the intended recovery behavior for this bug; the guard is limited to failed sessions with a missing persisted transcript path.

AI-assisted: yes. I used OpenClaw/Codex assistance and manually ran the reproduction and verification commands above.

target.title: feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall
target.body:
# PR 1 draft: Feishu agent message delete and recall

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall actions
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_message` tool for `list`, `delete`, `recall`, `read_receipts`, and `read_users`.
- Wire Feishu `messages` tool config through defaults, schema validation, plugin metadata, API exports, and runtime registration.
- Expose Telegram-like generic message actions for Feishu: `delete` and `unsend`.
- Track the latest bot-sent Feishu message per account/chat so natural-language requests like "delete your previous message" can work even when the model omits `messageId`.
- Document Feishu message-management permissions, Unix-second time filters, and delete/recall behavior.

## Related upstream work

- Closes / revives https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/51422
- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/52993
- Complements, but does not replace, inbound recall-event work in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/55225 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/55691
- Leaves Feishu reaction tool registration for a follow-up PR, because reaction fixes are already active in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Fixes compared with #52993

- `start_time` and `end_time` are documented and validated as Unix-second timestamp strings, matching Feishu's API, instead of ISO 8601 strings.
- `delete` and `recall` have distinct request paths and user-facing messages; delete no longer reuses recall semantics such as "cannot recall after two minutes".
- Missing Feishu scopes are surfaced with actionable messages, including `im:message.group_msg` for group-message deletion.

## Notes for reviewers

- Feishu delete behaves as a platform soft-delete/recall: follow-up reads can return `deleted: true`, `updated: true`, and empty content. Some clients may still show a deletion placeholder.
- The last-bot-message fallback is intentionally in-memory and scoped by account/chat with a 24-hour TTL. It only records messages sent by this bot process.
- The feature is gated by `tools.messages`, defaulting to `false` because message list/delete/recall/read-receipts are sensitive.

## Testing

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/message-tool.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts
# 4 files, 104 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts
# 2 files, 84 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 786 tests passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxlint extensions/feishu/src/sent-message-cache.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can delete or recall bot messages from a real group chat through the new `feishu_message` tool and generic `message(action="delete"|"unsend")` path.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw gateway from this branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scopes including `im:message:update`, `im:message:readonly`, and `im:message.group_msg`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enabled the opt-in message management gate for the Feishu test account, started the local gateway from this branch with `openclaw gateway run --force`, sent messages in the real Feishu group, first verified delete failed before `im:message.group_msg` was granted, granted the scope and restarted the gateway, sent a live bot reply, asked the bot to delete its previous message, then checked the deleted message through Feishu message read output.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output and redacted runtime notes from the real Feishu setup:

```text
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Feishu WebSocket ready at 2026-05-18T15:43:04+08:00

Initial group delete attempt:
  Feishu API error code=230027
  msg="need scope: im:message.group_msg"
  log_id=20260518150333B37818A84E685EE4FE60

After granting im:message.group_msg:
  sent message_id=om_x100b6f9fbdc2c8e4c3bb0f1440e9c1d
  im.message.delete response={"code":0,"msg":"success"}
  follow-up message read:
    deleted=true
    updated=true
    content_length=0

Natural-language group flow after last-bot-message fallback:
  user: delete your previous message
  agent called message(action="delete") without messageId
  fallback resolved last bot message in chat oc_* to om_*
  Feishu delete returned deleted=true
```

Observed result after fix: The bot replied in the Feishu group, deleted its own previous message after the required group-message scope was enabled, and Feishu readback showed the target message was deleted with empty content. The natural-language "delete your previous message" case also worked without the model supplying a `messageId`.

What was not tested: No additional gaps

target.title: Separate prompt surfaces by selected harness
target.body:
## Summary

This PR separates runtime-specific prompt fragments by explicit prompt surface so PI, CLI, ACP, native Codex app-server, and subagent prompts do not inherit each other's assumptions.

The core issue is not that PI's prompt is wrong. PI can keep the full OpenClaw control prompt. The problem is that native Codex already has its own harness prompt stack, so OpenClaw must add only the parts it owns instead of accidentally layering a second PI/Codex policy prompt on top of Codex.

## Why This Update

After the first implementation, I ran a second adversarial audit against the actual Codex native app-server prompt stack. That audit confirmed the surface split is the right abstraction, and it found a few places where the PR description needed to be explicit about double-prompt risks:

- Codex native app-server runs layer OpenClaw `developerInstructions` on top of Codex model/base/personality instructions.
- PI prompt text is valid for PI, but becomes risky when copied into Codex native or CLI prompt lanes.
- Some OpenClaw overrides are intentional and should remain, especially source-channel reply delivery and Codex-vs-OpenClaw delegation routing.
- Some broader OpenClaw prompt/context material should be narrowed or handled as follow-up because it can compete with Codex-native AGENTS, skills, and personality handling.

## Prompt Surface Model

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Runtime["Selected harness/runtime"] --> Surface["AgentPromptSurfaceKind"]
  Surface --> PI["pi_main"]
  Surface --> CLI["cli_backend"]
  Surface --> ACP["acp_backend"]
  Surface --> Codex["codex_app_server"]
  Surface --> Subagent["subagent"]

  PI --> PIPrompt["Full OpenClaw/PI system prompt"]
  CLI --> CLIPrompt["CLI backend prompt without PI fallback text"]
  ACP --> ACPPrompt["ACP-specific guidance"]
  Codex --> CodexPrompt["Codex developerInstructions only"]
  Subagent --> SubPrompt["Subagent-scoped guidance only"]
```

The boundary this PR introduces is simple: prompt routing follows the selected harness/runtime prompt surface, not provider-string matching and not a single shared global string bucket.

## Native Codex Layering

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  CodexModel["Codex model/base instructions"] --> CodexTurn["Codex native turn context"]
  CodexPersonality["Codex personality/model messages"] --> CodexTurn
  CodexApps["Codex apps/skills/plugins instructions"] --> CodexTurn
  CodexAgents["Codex AGENTS.md/project docs as contextual user content"] --> CodexTurn

  OpenClawDev["OpenClaw developerInstructions"] --> CodexTurn
  OpenClawTools["OpenClaw dynamic tools"] --> CodexTurn
  UserPrompt["OpenClaw user/source prompt"] --> CodexTurn

  CodexTurn --> Model["Responses request"]
```

OpenClaw does not replace Codex `baseInstructions` in this PR. It uses Codex's `developerInstructions` lane for OpenClaw-owned behavior. That is the right lane, but it means we have to keep OpenClaw additions narrow.

## What Changes

- Adds a small prompt-surface helper and public plugin command guidance types for scoped agent prompt guidance.
- Keeps legacy `agentPromptGuidance: string[]` behavior while allowing structured `{ text, surfaces }` entries.
- Scopes Codex native command guidance to the main PI/OpenClaw agent prompt surface instead of subagent or CLI prompt lanes.
- Applies the same surface-scoped command guidance when compacted embedded runs rebuild their system prompt.
- Keeps PI's empty-tool fallback intact, while CLI backend prompts no longer mention PI or OpenClaw tool workflow hints when no OpenClaw tools are injected.
- Makes Codex app-server developer reply instructions conditional on `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, so automatic visible replies are not told to use `message` unconditionally.
- Keeps native Codex context priority sane by moving OpenClaw skills and non-`AGENTS.md` bootstrap context out of Codex app-server `developerInstructions` and into turn input reference context.
- Keeps Codex observability honest by using the same decorated turn prompt for `turn/start`, `llm_input`, trajectory `context.compiled`, and `prompt.submitted`.
- Incorporates adversarial-review hardening: structured prompt-surface validation now follows the file's lazy-init pattern for bundled startup safety, and subagent-scoped guidance renders even when ACP is disabled.

## Scenarios

| Scenario | Before | After / Intended Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| PI has no explicit tool lines | PI fallback text is valid and should remain | PI still renders the OpenClaw tool fallback |
| CLI backend has no OpenClaw tools | CLI could inherit PI/OpenClaw fallback wording | CLI says no OpenClaw tool list is injected and uses only backend tools |
| Subagent prompt is built | Global Codex command guidance could appear in child prompts | Subagent receives only subagent-scoped guidance |
| Native Codex automatic source reply | Codex could be told to use `message` even when Codex delivery is automatic | Automatic mode says to use the active Codex delivery path |
| Native Codex `message_tool_only` source reply | Codex final-answer habit can conflict with OpenClaw channel delivery | OpenClaw intentionally overrides and tells Codex to use `message` |
| Native Codex delegation | Codex native `spawn_agent` and OpenClaw `sessions_spawn` can compete | Codex subagents use native `spawn_agent`; OpenClaw/ACP delegation uses `sessions_spawn` |
| ACP backend prompt | ACP-specific docs/snapshots can become the oracle for Codex app-server behavior | ACP remains its own prompt surface and does not define native Codex behavior |

## Codex Conflict Audit

These are the specific conflict classes checked against the actual Codex native prompt stack:

### 1. Codex base/personality versus OpenClaw GPT-5 overlay

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A["Codex base/model/personality prompt"] --> C["Native Codex run"]
  B["OpenClaw GPT-5 behavior/personality overlay"] --> C
  C --> Risk["Risk: duplicate style/execution policy"]
```

Severity: high for native Codex if left broad.

Recommendation: native Codex should let Codex own base/model/personality behavior. OpenClaw should keep only OpenClaw-owned routing, channel delivery, and runtime guidance in `developerInstructions`.

### 2. Visible reply delivery versus Codex final-answer behavior

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant OpenClaw
  participant Codex
  participant Channel

  User->>OpenClaw: Source-channel request
  OpenClaw->>Codex: developerInstructions with delivery mode
  alt message_tool_only
    Codex->>OpenClaw: message(action=send)
    OpenClaw->>Channel: visible reply
  else automatic
    Codex->>OpenClaw: normal Codex delivery path
    OpenClaw->>Channel: route once
  end
```

Severity: medium-high, but intentional.

Recommendation: keep the OpenClaw override only for `message_tool_only`. Automatic mode should stay neutral, which this PR now enforces.

### 3. Codex native subagents versus OpenClaw sessions

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Ask["Delegation request"] --> IsCodex{"Codex-native subagent?"}
  IsCodex -->|Yes| Spawn["Use Codex spawn_agent"]
  IsCodex -->|No, OpenClaw or ACP session| Sessions["Use OpenClaw sessions_spawn"]
```

Severity: medium-high, but intentional.

Recommendation: keep this disambiguation in the Codex app-server developer instructions. Without it, Codex sees two delegation surfaces and can choose the wrong one.

### 4. Lightweight Codex context versus AGENTS.md

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Light["OpenClaw lightweight context"] --> Config["project_doc_max_bytes = 0"]
  Config --> CodexDocs["Codex native AGENTS.md loading disabled"]
  Bootstrap["OpenClaw bootstrap text"] --> Claim["Claims Codex loads AGENTS.md natively"]
  CodexDocs --> Conflict["Conflict"]
  Claim --> Conflict
```

Severity: high when lightweight mode is used.

Recommendation: do not claim Codex loads AGENTS.md if the thread config disables project docs, or inject equivalent scoped AGENTS content when lightweight mode intentionally suppresses native docs. This is a prompt-surface correctness issue, not a PI prompt issue.

### 5. Skills and user-editable context priority

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  OpenClawSkills["OpenClaw skillsSnapshot.prompt"] --> Dev["Codex developerInstructions"]
  CodexSkills["Codex native skills instructions"] --> DevBundle["Codex developer bundle"]
  WorkspaceContext["SOUL/MEMORY/HEARTBEAT/project files"] --> Dev
  Dev --> Risk["Risk: duplicated or elevated user-editable context"]
  DevBundle --> Risk
```

Severity: medium.

Recommendation: keep OpenClaw-only context, but avoid turning generic user-editable context into Codex developer policy unless that priority is intentional. This is a good follow-up area after the prompt-surface routing split is in place.

### 6. Legacy plugin guidance compatibility

Severity: medium-low.

Legacy string guidance remains global for backwards compatibility. New structured entries can be scoped by surface. This PR preserves old plugin behavior while giving new prompt callers a precise path.

## Scope Boundary

This PR should be reviewed as the prompt-surface split:

- In scope: explicit prompt surface kinds, scoped plugin command guidance, PI/CLI/subagent/Codex developer-instruction routing, and visible-reply delivery wording.
- In scope to harden before merge if needed: any direct contradiction that makes a non-PI surface render PI/OpenClaw workflow text.
- Follow-up: deeper native Codex context priority decisions that are not required for this split, including any decision to suppress OpenClaw GPT-5 personality overlay on native Codex by default.
- Out of scope: harness selection policy, model routing, auth profile routing, Codex app-server transport behavior, ACP runtime behavior, and GitNexus indexing freshness.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: prompt routing now follows the selected runtime prompt surface instead of leaking PI/Codex/CLI/subagent assumptions through shared strings.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, branch `codex/prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, using the repo's TypeScript runtime loader (`node --import tsx`) against the actual prompt builders.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pwd
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { buildAgentSystemPrompt } from './src/agents/system-prompt.ts';
import { buildCliAgentSystemPrompt } from './src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.ts';
import { resolveAgentPromptSurfaceForSessionKey } from './src/agents/prompt-surface.ts';
import { buildDeveloperInstructions } from './extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts';
import { clearPluginCommands, registerPluginCommand } from './src/plugins/commands.ts';

const workspaceDir = process.cwd();
clearPluginCommands();
registerPluginCommand('probe-plugin', {
  name: 'probe_cmd',
  description: 'Probe command',
  agentPromptGuidance: [
    { text: 'PI main probe guidance.', surfaces: ['pi_main'] },
    { text: 'CLI backend probe guidance.', surfaces: ['cli_backend'] },
    { text: 'Codex app-server probe guidance.', surfaces: ['codex_app_server'] },
    { text: 'ACP backend probe guidance.', surfaces: ['acp_backend'] },
    { text: 'Subagent probe guidance.', surfaces: ['subagent'] },
  ],
  handler: async () => ({ text: 'ok' }),
});
const piPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: 'pi_main', nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['PI main probe guidance.'] });
const cliPrompt = buildCliAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, tools: [], modelDisplay: 'test/model' });
const acpSurface = resolveAgentPromptSurfaceForSessionKey('agent:codex:acp:probe');
const acpPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: acpSurface, nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['ACP backend probe guidance.'] });
const subagentPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: 'subagent', nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['Subagent probe guidance.'] });
const baseCodexParams = {
  agentDir: workspaceDir,
  workspaceDir,
  provider: 'openai-codex-responses',
  modelId: 'gpt-5.4-codex',
  config: {},
  prompt: 'probe prompt',
};
const messageToolOnlyDeveloper = buildDeveloperInstructions({
  ...baseCodexParams,
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'message_tool_only',
});
const automaticDeveloper = buildDeveloperInstructions({
  ...baseCodexParams,
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'automatic',
});

const probe = {
  cwd: workspaceDir,
  piHasPiFallback: piPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  cliHasPiFallback: cliPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  cliHasBackendFallback: cliPrompt.includes('No OpenClaw tool list is injected for this runtime prompt surface'),
  cliHasLongWaitHint: cliPrompt.includes('For long waits'),
  cliHasSessionsSpawnHint: cliPrompt.includes('Larger work: use `sessions_spawn`'),
  cliHasPollingHint: cliPrompt.includes('Do not poll `subagents list` / `sessions_list` in a loop'),
  cliHasCliScopedGuidance: cliPrompt.includes('CLI backend probe guidance.'),
  cliHasPiScopedGuidance: cliPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  acpSurfaceFromSessionKey: acpSurface,
  acpHasAcpScopedGuidance: acpPrompt.includes('ACP backend probe guidance.'),
  acpHasPiScopedGuidance: acpPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  acpHasPiFallback: acpPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  subagentHasSubagentGuidance: subagentPrompt.includes('Subagent probe guidance.'),
  subagentHasPiGuidance: subagentPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  messageToolOnlySaysUseMessage: messageToolOnlyDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies: use `message`'),
  automaticSaysUseMessage: automaticDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies: use `message`'),
  automaticUsesActiveDeliveryPath: automaticDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies should use the active Codex delivery path'),
  codexHasCodexScopedGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('Codex app-server probe guidance.'),
  codexHasPiScopedGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  codexHasNativeSpawnAgentGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('Use Codex native `spawn_agent` for Codex subagents'),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(probe, null, 2));
clearPluginCommands();
EOF
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the live prompt probe:

```text
/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation
{
  "cwd": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation",
  "piHasPiFallback": true,
  "cliHasPiFallback": false,
  "cliHasBackendFallback": true,
  "cliHasLongWaitHint": false,
  "cliHasSessionsSpawnHint": false,
  "cliHasPollingHint": false,
  "cliHasCliScopedGuidance": true,
  "cliHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "acpSurfaceFromSessionKey": "acp_backend",
  "acpHasAcpScopedGuidance": true,
  "acpHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "acpHasPiFallback": false,
  "subagentHasSubagentGuidance": true,
  "subagentHasPiGuidance": false,
  "messageToolOnlySaysUseMessage": true,
  "automaticSaysUseMessage": false,
  "automaticUsesActiveDeliveryPath": true,
  "codexHasCodexScopedGuidance": true,
  "codexHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "codexHasNativeSpawnAgentGuidance": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: the rendered PI prompt still includes the PI empty-tool fallback; the rendered CLI backend prompt does not include PI wording or OpenClaw long-wait/session-spawn/polling workflow hints when no tool list is injected; ACP session keys resolve to the `acp_backend` surface and render ACP-scoped guidance without PI guidance or fallback text; CLI, subagent, and Codex app-server scoped command guidance render only on their matching surfaces; Codex `message_tool_only` developer instructions still say to use `message`, while Codex `automatic` developer instructions use the active delivery path instead of a hard `message` instruction.

What was not tested: full repository test suite, live Codex app-server connection to Codex Desktop, GitNexus refreshed index.

## Folded Codex Context Priority Fix

The brief #83555 draft follow-up was folded back into this PR. The original prompt-surface split remains the canonical change set, and the folded commits address the Codex-native double-prompt concern directly:

- OpenClaw skills and non-`AGENTS.md` bootstrap context no longer enter Codex app-server `developerInstructions`.
- Native Codex still receives that OpenClaw context in the lower-priority turn input reference lane.
- Lightweight cron/project-doc suppression remains intact.
- The decorated turn prompt is the prompt reported to `turn/start`, `llm_input`, trajectory `context.compiled`, and `prompt.submitted`.

Additional local validation after the fold-in:

```bash
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results: 199 tests passed, 126 tests passed, 4 focused Vitest shards passed, and `git diff --check` passed from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`.

## Post-Rebase Update

Rebased onto upstream `main` at `419eea24622b8649dd98155734a01f972463e300`, including upstream #83561 (`fix(codex): stop forcing code-mode-only turns`). This PR preserves that upstream behavior: Codex code mode is enabled, `features.code_mode_only` defaults to `false`, and explicit thread config can still opt into code-mode-only.

Current head proof after rebase:

```text
OpenClaw prompt-surface Codex context proof
{
  "cwd": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation",
  "head": "ad0cb3bf7fd3",
  "developerContainsOpenClawSkills": false,
  "developerContainsOpenClawWorkspaceContext": false,
  "developerVisibleReplyInstruction": "Preserve channel/session context. Visible channel replies should use the active Codex delivery path; do not describe would-reply.",
  "sourceBuildsTurnInputContextFromSkills": true,
  "sourcePrependsOpenClawContextToTurnInput": true,
  "sourceKeepsDecoratedPromptObservable": true,
  "sourceSkipsOpenClawContextForLightweightCron": true,
  "reportCountsDemotedSkillsOnlyWhenInjected": true
}
```

Additional post-rebase validation from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`:

```bash
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
DOCS_I18N_GLOSSARY_BASE=upstream/main pnpm check:docs
pnpm format:docs:check
node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
OPENCLAW_ADDITIONAL_BOUNDARY_SHARD=1/4 OPENCLAW_ADDITIONAL_BOUNDARY_CONCURRENCY=1 node scripts/run-additional-boundary-checks.mjs
git diff --check
```

Results: 200 Codex app-server tests passed, 234 prompt/registry/compaction tests passed, docs check passed against upstream main, docs formatting passed, conflict-marker check passed, additional boundary shard 1/4 passed after snapshot refresh, and `git diff --check` passed.

## Verification

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
git diff --check
```

- Focused prompt/registry/compaction/Codex/plugin-SDK tests passed 5 Vitest shards in 31.68s after the adversarial-review hardening fixes.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Adversarial Sweep

Four read-only adversarial review passes covered prompt-surface correctness, lifecycle/compaction routing, tests/mocks, and public SDK/API compatibility. Two findings survived manual verification and were fixed in this head:

- P1 bundled startup safety: avoid a module-scope prompt-surface `Set` in `command-registration.ts`, matching the file's existing lazy-init TDZ guard pattern.
- P2 subagent guidance placement: render subagent-scoped command guidance independently of ACP-specific routing text, with regression coverage for ACP-disabled subagent prompts.

A second Codex-native conflict audit compared OpenClaw's Codex bridge to the actual Codex app-server prompt stack. That audit produced the scenarios documented above: native Codex owns base/model/personality/AGENTS/native skills, while OpenClaw should own delivery routing, dynamic-tool routing, ACP/session disambiguation, and genuinely OpenClaw-only context.

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex no longer throws away the only saved app-server thread when the dynamic tool catalog changes. Instead, it keeps reusable Codex thread bindings per dynamic-tool fingerprint, so owner/non-owner tool availability flips start each catalog once and then resume the matching native thread.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. That meant default Codex app-server config could still register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint included the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools are filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change can alter the registered dynamic tool names. Before this PR, a mismatch cleared the single stored Codex binding, so alternating owner/non-owner turns could continuously create fresh native Codex sessions.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now only reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` can persist alternate Codex app-server thread bindings for the same OpenClaw session.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` selects a binding compatible with the current dynamic-tool fingerprint. If none exists, it starts a new binding without deleting other compatible bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` reselects the startup binding after dynamic tools are built, so context/startup decisions use the matching per-catalog Codex thread.
- Owner-only tools stay filtered for non-owner Codex thread starts. This avoids exposing owner-only schemas to non-owner turns.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching between owner and non-owner senders could change dynamic tool registration and repeatedly replace the saved Codex native thread binding.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Owner-only schemas are not sent to non-owner Codex thread starts.
- Owner and non-owner dynamic-tool catalogs get separate reusable Codex thread bindings: first flip starts the second catalog, later flips resume the matching existing thread.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not continuously replace Codex native sessions.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw checkout on May 18, 2026, Node `v22.22.0`, Codex CLI logged in with ChatGPT auth. Private home/workspace paths and auth details are redacted in the older live-run transcript below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: On PR head `9f5c2eae749ff332c1b4dda50d7c04203968a4cf`, ran a production-code runtime probe against `startOrResumeThread` and session-binding using owner tools `[message, cron]` and non-owner tools `[message]`; the current rebased head `cf5ea6514b48c784110297db8e43ba553e0f9035` has the same 10-file runtime diff after a clean rebase onto latest `main`. Earlier on this PR branch, ran two real `pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 ...` turns through the Codex app-server harness.
- Evidence after fix: Embedded terminal/runtime output below includes the current-head production-code JSON probe plus the earlier live Codex app-server command transcript and stdout.
- Observed result after fix: The owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume `thread-1` and `thread-2` on later flips; the non-owner `thread/start` payload contains only `message`, not `cron`, and the live Codex app-server smoke completed two turns through the real harness/auth path.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip was exercised through the production Codex thread lifecycle code path in the runtime probe below, and the local live Codex session verified the actual app-server harness/auth path.

### Production-code runtime probe on current head

This directly exercised the production `startOrResumeThread` and session-binding code on `9f5c2eae`, using owner tools `[message, cron]` and non-owner tools `[message]`:

```json
{
  "requestMethods": [
    "thread/start",
    "thread/start",
    "thread/resume",
    "thread/resume"
  ],
  "startDynamicTools": [
    [
      "message",
      "cron"
    ],
    [
      "message"
    ]
  ],
  "resumeThreadIds": [
    "thread-1",
    "thread-2"
  ],
  "storedBindings": [
    {
      "threadId": "thread-2",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    },
    {
      "threadId": "thread-1",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result: the owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume `thread-1` and `thread-2` on later flips. The non-owner `thread/start` payload only contained `message`, not `cron`.

### Live Codex app-server smoke

Earlier on this PR branch, I ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns with the same session id. That verified the actual app-server harness/auth path and same-thread resume behavior:

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"
```

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are runtime process/session churn in the Codex app-server path. Terminal/runtime proof is the relevant artifact.

## Tests / Proof

Current-head verification after the per-catalog binding update:

~~~text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
cf5ea6514b48c784110297db8e43ba553e0f9035

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output

$ env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/Users/stainlu/.npm-global/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  61 passed (61)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  177 passed (177)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 49.77s

$ pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/Users/stainlu/.npm-global/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin pnpm check:test-types
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
# exited 0
~~~

Specific regressions covered by this PR:

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts`: alternate dynamic-tool thread bindings persist beside the same OpenClaw session file.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: non-owner Codex thread starts omit owner-only dynamic tools, and owner/non-owner flips start each tool catalog once before resuming matching thread bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment; the focused affected suites and the type-test gate above are green locally. The latest push also triggers exact-head GitHub CI.

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: 1a8e618dc10763685c3d5739abeab70e7a2be8f3.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts\n\n RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517\n\n Test Files  1 passed (1)\n      Tests  78 passed (78)\n   Duration  12.41s\n\n[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.72s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:

```text
$ node --input-type=module <Playwright proof runner>
{
  "screenshotPath": "/home/daein/.openclaw/workspace/logs/pr-watch/75727-ui-proof-2ce0267.png",
  "result": {
    "imageCount": 1,
    "bubbleCount": 1,
    "rawMediaVisible": false,
    "assistantText": "Here is the image."
  }
}
```

Proof setup: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI renderMessageGroup implementation from this PR branch in system Chromium via Playwright. The message shape matched the normalized gateway result after the raw MEDIA transcript turn is replaced: one assistant text block plus one input_image content block, with no MEDIA directive left in visible text.

- What was not tested: I did not run a fresh browser visual smoke for this exact head; the current-head proof covers the real transcript/media rewrite behavior that ClawSweeper flagged.
